varieties.name: Nellie Kelly Grafted Black
varieties.origin: Australia, developed by Nellie Kelly plant nursery. Modern grafted cultivar (post-1950s).
varieties.traits: Hybrid purple passionfruit grafted onto Passiflora caerulea (blue passionfruit) rootstock.
varieties.yield_notes: Very productive: 200-400 fruits per season. Prolific bearer over 8-10 year lifespan.

varieties.name: Purple Passionfruit (Standard Edulis)
varieties.origin: Native to southern Brazil through Paraguay to northern Argentina. Cultivated since 1800s.
varieties.traits: Open-pollinated, heirloom Passiflora edulis forma edulis.
varieties.yield_notes: Moderate to high productivity, depending on conditions.
varieties.availability: Queensland nurseries, seed suppliers, Daley's Fruit Tree Nursery.

varieties.name: Misty Gem (Seedling Selection)
varieties.origin: Australia. Commercial selection developed for Australian conditions. Note: Original grafted variety declining; seedling selections available.
varieties.traits: Open-pollinated seedling from Misty Gem cultivar. Purple/black passionfruit.
varieties.yield_notes: High productivity and excellent fruit quality.
varieties.availability: Daley's Fruit Tree Nursery, Queensland specialty nurseries.

varieties.name: Supersweet (Black/Purple)
varieties.origin: Australia. Hybrid developed from Burringbar breeding work (northern NSW). Burringbar Hybrid crossed to create Supersweet selections.
varieties.traits: Hybrid with enhanced sweetness and disease tolerance.
varieties.yield_notes: High yield with good disease tolerance.
varieties.availability: Queensland nurseries, Daley's Fruit Tree Nursery.

varieties.name: Sweetheart
varieties.origin: Australia. Commercial selection (APIA-owned variety). Note: Declining vigor reported by Queensland Alliance for Agriculture and Food Innovation.
varieties.traits: Black passionfruit, grafted commercial variety.
varieties.yield_notes: Excellent productivity - one of highest yielding black varieties.

varieties.name: Tango
varieties.origin: Australia. New breeding selection from National Passionfruit Breeding Program.
varieties.traits: Modern hybrid with improved agronomic performance.
varieties.yield_notes: Developed for improved yield and disease resistance.
varieties.availability: Select Queensland nurseries, commercial suppliers.

varieties.name: Flamenco
varieties.origin: Australia. New breeding selection from National Passionfruit Breeding Program.
varieties.traits: Modern hybrid purple variety.
varieties.yield_notes: Bred for improved productivity and consumer appeal.
varieties.availability: Queensland nurseries, commercial suppliers, Arbour Grove Nursery.

varieties.name: Frederick
varieties.origin: Developed in Australia. Cold-hardy purple selection.
varieties.traits: Cold-hardy purple hybrid.
varieties.yield_notes: Good productivity with enhanced cold tolerance.

varieties.name: Panama Red
varieties.origin: South America (Panama region). Cultivated in Australia, particularly Queensland.
varieties.traits: Hybrid variety - more tropical than black passionfruit. Often grafted onto disease-resistant rootstock.
varieties.yield_notes: Heavy cropping, particularly autumn through spring. Vigorous growth. 100-150 fruits per season.

varieties.name: Pandora (Panama Red)
varieties.origin: Australia. Commercial selection of Panama Red type.
varieties.traits: Hybrid Panama variety. Vigorous, more tropical than black.
varieties.yield_notes: Heavy cropping, self-pollinating. One of main Panama varieties grown commercially in Queensland.
varieties.availability: Queensland nurseries, Fruit Tree Cottage, Ross Creek Tropicals, Fruitopia Nursery.

varieties.name: McGuffie's Red
varieties.origin: Australia. Named variety selection.
varieties.traits: Panama-style hybrid.
varieties.yield_notes: Good productivity.
varieties.availability: Specialty nurseries, limited availability.

varieties.name: Pink Passionfruit (SuperSweet AV1)
varieties.origin: Australia. Recent breeding development (APIA-owned).
varieties.traits: Hybrid grafted onto Fusarium-resistant rootstock.
varieties.yield_notes: Crops most of the year. High-quality fruit production.
varieties.availability: Daley's Fruit Tree Nursery, specialty Queensland nurseries (APIA-restricted).

varieties.name: Panama Gold
varieties.origin: South America/Australia. Developed for Australian conditions.
varieties.traits: Hybrid, more tropical than black. Grafted onto hardy rootstock.
varieties.yield_notes: 100-150 fruits per season. Heavy cropper.
varieties.availability: Garden Express, Queensland nurseries, Daley's Fruit Tree Nursery.

varieties.name: Yellow Passionfruit (Standard Flavicarpa)
varieties.origin: Possibly Amazon basin of Brazil. Cultivated since 1800s-1900s.
varieties.traits: Open-pollinated Passiflora edulis f. flavicarpa. More tropical than purple.
varieties.yield_notes: Higher yields than purple varieties. More vigorous vine. 200-300 fruits per plant per season.
varieties.availability: Queensland nurseries, seed suppliers.

varieties.name: Hawaiian Yellow
varieties.origin: Developed in Hawaii from South American stock.
varieties.traits: Yellow passionfruit selection.
varieties.yield_notes: Vigorous and productive.
varieties.availability: Specialty nurseries, Daley's Fruit Tree Nursery.

varieties.name: Sweet Sunrise Yellow
varieties.origin: Florida, United States. Modern selection.
varieties.traits: Yellow variety selected for sweetness.
varieties.yield_notes: Good productivity in warm conditions.
varieties.availability: Specialty nurseries, online suppliers.

varieties.name: Sweet Granadilla (Passiflora ligularis)
varieties.origin: Native to Andean highlands of Colombia, Ecuador, Peru, Bolivia. Cultivated since pre-Columbian times (1500s or earlier).
varieties.traits: Heirloom species. Different from P. edulis. Subtropical to mountain species.
varieties.yield_notes: Moderate yield. Fruit in 2-3 years from planting.
varieties.availability: Daley's Fruit Tree Nursery, Ross Creek Tropicals, specialty suppliers.

varieties.name: Banana Passionfruit (Passiflora tarminiana/mollissima)
varieties.origin: Native to Andean valleys. Cultivated historically in cooler climates.
varieties.traits: Heirloom species. More cold-tolerant than edulis. WARNING: Considered invasive weed in some Australian regions.
varieties.yield_notes: Productive. Fruits ripen autumn-winter.
varieties.availability: Limited due to weed restrictions. Check local regulations before purchasing.

varieties.name: Giant Granadilla (Passiflora quadrangularis)
varieties.origin: Tropical Central and South America. Cultivated historically.
varieties.traits: Heirloom species. Vigorous tropical vine.
varieties.yield_notes: Moderate, requires hand-pollination for best set.
varieties.availability: Daley's Fruit Tree Nursery, specialty tropical fruit suppliers.

varieties.name: Purple Passionfruit (Passiflora edulis) 🌱
varieties.origin: Native to South America. Introduced to India in early 1900s via Nilgiris, Coorg, and Malabar regions of southern India. Well-established in tropical monsoon climates.

varieties.name: Yellow Passionfruit (Passiflora edulis f. flavicarpa) 🌱
varieties.origin: Amazon basin. Introduced to India from Sri Lanka several decades ago, quickly adapted to tropical monsoon climates lowlands.

varieties.name: Kaveri 🧬
varieties.origin: India. Developed 1986 at Central Horticultural Experiment Station (CHES), Chettalli, Indian Institute of Horticultural Research (IIHR), Karnataka.
varieties.traits: F1 hybrid - cross between purple and yellow varieties. India's premier commercial passionfruit variety.
varieties.yield_notes: Very high yielding: 40-60 fruits per vine per annum, 200 tonnes/hectare over 3-year period. Among highest yielders at CHES trials (10.24 kg per vine).

varieties.name: PF-2 🧬
varieties.origin: India. Elite breeding line from CHES Chettalli, IIHR.
varieties.traits: Advanced breeding selection under development.
varieties.yield_notes: Very good - 8.14 kg per vine in trials.
varieties.availability: CHES Chettalli, IIHR research stations, may become commercially available.

varieties.name: PF-3 (Coorg Yellow Selection) 🧬
varieties.origin: India. Seedling selection of Coorg yellow at CHES Chettalli.
varieties.traits: Elite yellow selection from regional Coorg variety.
varieties.yield_notes: Very high - 9.07 kg per vine in trials.
varieties.availability: CHES Chettalli, IIHR Bangalore, Karnataka/tropical monsoon climates research stations.

varieties.name: PF-7 (Thrissur Yellow Selection) 🧬
varieties.origin: India. Seedling selection of Thrissur yellow at CHES Chettalli, tropical monsoon climates.
varieties.traits: Elite tropical monsoon climates yellow selection.
varieties.yield_notes: Highest yielder in CHES trials: 10.94 kg per vine - the best performer.
varieties.availability: tropical monsoon climates Agricultural University, Pineapple Research Station Vazhakulam, CHES Chettalli, tropical monsoon climates state nurseries.

varieties.name: Sweet Granadilla (Passiflora ligularis)
varieties.origin: Andean highlands. Introduced to India.
varieties.traits: Heirloom species, different from P. edulis.
varieties.yield_notes: Moderate, fruits in 2-3 years.
varieties.availability: Specialty suppliers, limited in tropical monsoon climates.

varieties.name: Giant Granadilla (Passiflora quadrangularis)
varieties.origin: Tropical America. Rarely cultivated in India.
varieties.traits: Heirloom species. Vigorous tropical vine.
varieties.yield_notes: Moderate, requires hand-pollination.
varieties.availability: Very limited in India, specialty collectors.

varieties.name: Water Lemon (Passiflora laurifolia)
varieties.origin: Tropical America.
varieties.traits: Specialty species.
varieties.yield_notes: Moderate productivity.
varieties.availability: Very limited, specialty collectors only.

varieties.name: General Growing Notes
varieties.growing_notes: Yellow varieties and hybrids generally show better resistance to fusarium wilt and nematodes than purple varieties. Grafted varieties on resistant rootstock significantly reduce disease problems.
